_comment: Phase-1 single-protocol audit results land here, per-seed, read from metrics.npy

published_reference.source: S-Mamba paper table (propagated from iTransformer Table 9)
S-Mamba paper table (propagated from iTransformer Table 9)

results.note: Beats every method on this cell incl. StructMamba 0.0738, DMamba published 0.074, S-Mamba published 0.076 - self-run 2023 baseline tops the 2026 'SOTA' table
Again tops the cell: S-Mamba pub 0.084, DMamba pub 0.089, MultiRel 0.1055, iT 0.1248. Published row inflated 40%.

HIGH SEED VARIANCE (0.129-0.207, 60% spread) - instability at longer horizon. Mean 0.1609 vs MultiRel 0.1589: CrossFormer's short-horizon lead vanishes at pred-48. Worst seed matches the published row, suggesting published value may be a single bad run.
Published row inflated 39%. vs MultiRel 0.1807, iT 0.2011.

results.status: OOM at batch 32 on 8GB RTX 3070 (forward pass, TwoStageAttention); batch-16 retry staged (fixup_P08.sh), runs after TimesNet grid

results.note: 44% seed spread at N=883 (0.0579-0.0836): large-N instability visible even at pred-12. Best seed would top the table (DMamba pub 0.059); mean 0.0677 sits behind MultiRel 0.0633. Published row inflated 28%.

published_reference.source: ours; comparisons: DMamba pub 0.059, S-Mamba pub 0.063, MultiRel 0.0633, iT 0.0668, CrossFormer self-run 0.0677

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from results/*SM_P07_12*_sm_{0,1,2}; all seeds post duplicate-kill, clean checkpoint dir
results.note: Statistically ties DMamba's published 0.059 ON THE CANONICAL PROTOCOL (DMamba's protocol differs/stronger). Tightest seeds of any method on this cell (std 0.0001) - stability story confirmed at N=883.

Published row inflated 3.1x - the most extreme case found. Mean 0.1310 beats S-Mamba pub 0.137 and DMamba pub 0.143; even the worst seed (0.1794) beats every canonical-protocol method (MultiRel 0.2487, iT 0.3115). CrossFormer PEMS04 grid COMPLETE: 0.0679/0.0783/0.1609/0.1310.

published_reference.source: ours; comparisons: DMamba pub 0.077, S-Mamba pub 0.081, MultiRel 0.0890, iT 0.0969

results.note: Ties S-Mamba published (0.081) on canonical protocol; DMamba pub 0.077 ahead by ~6%. std 0.0003.
Extreme seed variance at 24/48/96 (up to 60%). Cross-box check: earlier A4000 run gave pred-24 0.0874 / pred-96 0.1997 - seed lottery dominates CrossFormer on PEMS03.
Extreme seed variance at 24/48/96 (up to 60%). Cross-box check: earlier A4000 run gave pred-24 0.0874 / pred-96 0.1997 - seed lottery dominates CrossFormer on PEMS03.
Extreme seed variance at 24/48/96 (up to 60%). Cross-box check: earlier A4000 run gave pred-24 0.0874 / pred-96 0.1997 - seed lottery dominates CrossFormer on PEMS03.
Extreme seed variance at 24/48/96 (up to 60%). Cross-box check: earlier A4000 run gave pred-24 0.0874 / pred-96 0.1997 - seed lottery dominates CrossFormer on PEMS03.

published_reference.source: ours; DMamba pub 0.096, S-Mamba pub 0.093, MultiRel 0.1356, iT 0.1572

results.note: Well ahead of canonical baselines; behind Mamba published rows at this horizon (their protocol is stronger).
First cell where published row matches self-run mean (0.139 vs 0.1358). 43% seed spread persists. At N=883 CrossFormer collapses: StructMamba 0.0817 / MultiRel 0.0890 far ahead.

First DEFLATION case: published (0.112) slightly better than self-run mean (0.1200, -7%) - within seed noise. TimesNet's published rows look roughly honest, unlike CrossFormer's. Audit reports both directions.
Second deflation case: published 0.087 better than self-run 0.0955 (-9%). TimesNet published rows consistently honest-to-favorable; CrossFormer's were the outlier.

published_reference.source: ours; DMamba pub 0.131, S-Mamba pub 0.117, MultiRel 0.2166, iT 0.2485

results.note: 20%+ ahead of canonical baselines; Mamba published rows (stronger protocol) lead at long horizon on PEMS07.

results.provenance: CLEAN RERUN (rerun_p04.sh, single instance verified); values bit-identical to dual-instance era - contamination confirmed benign (identical seeds -> identical checkpoints)

results.note: Third deflation case (published favorable by 10%); 14% seed spread.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from SDMamba/results/SMPUB_P04_12_*_{0,1,2}; their script verbatim, itr 1->3
results.note: REPRODUCES (0.0726 vs published 0.076, slightly better). But even at 4x width it loses the cell to canonical-protocol CrossFormer (0.0679). Canonical-config S-Mamba regime runs next.

results.config: INTERMEDIATE capacity probe (e4/d512/lr1e-3) - NOT the official published config (which is d1024/e4/lr5e-4)

published_reference.source: iTransformer paper row as cited by DMamba

results.note: Key negative: doubling d_model 256->512 changes nothing (0.0881 vs canonical 0.0887). The published 0.078 must come from d1024 and/or lr - exact-official-script reruns (tag ITP) launched to test.

results.config: published-style e4/d512/lr1e-3 (official PEMS03 script's pred-12 block)
results.note: Three-way agreement at short horizon: published 0.071 = canonical d256 0.0694 = published-config 0.0692. iT protocol inflation is a LONG-horizon phenomenon (published 0.164 vs canonical 0.2755 at pred-96) - those cells are queued on this box.
Published inflated 1.5x; still far behind StructMamba 0.1185 / MultiRel 0.1356 at N=883.

results.status: OOM at batch 8 on 8GB RTX 5060; batch-4 retry staged (fixup_P07.sh) after TimesNet grid

results.note: Exact reproduction (0.0843 vs 0.084). vs canonical-protocol: CrossFormer 0.0783 still wins the cell; StructMamba 0.0919.
Published favorable by 13% - TimesNet's cited rows consistently at or better than canonical reproduction (4th case).

Capacity decomposition: canonical d256 0.1002 -> d512/e4 0.0972 -> published 0.093. Capacity explains ~half the published gap at pred-24; per-horizon lr/e5 tuning presumably the rest (exact-script runs queued on dual-A4000).
Published favorable by 13%; 20% seed spread (0.108-0.130) - TimesNet variance also non-trivial at longer horizons.

Reproduces favorably (0.1006 vs pub 0.115). FIRST CELL where Mamba genuinely leads with verified numbers: beats DMamba pub 0.113, StructMamba 0.1297, CrossFormer 0.1609. Canonical-config row pending to decompose capacity vs architecture.

Capacity does NOTHING at pred-48 (canonical 0.1651 -> d512/e4 0.1639) yet published row is 0.125. The 24% residual gap must be per-horizon tuning (official script mixes e5/lr1e-4) or non-reproducibility - exact-script blocks staged on this box.

DIVERGED 3/3 SEEDS (MSE>1.0). Naive capacity scaling at lr1e-3 catastrophically unstable at long horizon - exactly the Limitation-3 instability the MultiRel/StructMamba stability analysis predicts. The published 0.164 therefore requires the official script's per-horizon lr1e-4/e5 settings; exact-script verification staged (ITEX). Canonical d256 converges fine (0.2755).

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from TSLibAudit/results/*ITP_PEMS04_12*; config verified in result dirname (dm1024_el4_df1024)
results.note: EXACT official script (d1024/e4/lr5e-4/use_norm0, dirname-verified) DOES NOT REPRODUCE published row. Identical to canonical d256 (0.0887) - capacity contributes nothing on PEMS04 either.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from TSLibAudit/results/*ITP_PEMS04_24*; config verified in result dirname (dm1024_el4_df1024)
results.note: EXACT official script (d1024/e4/lr5e-4/use_norm0, dirname-verified) DOES NOT REPRODUCE published row.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from TSLibAudit/results/*ITP_PEMS04_48*; config verified in result dirname (dm1024_el4_df1024)
results.note: EXACT official script (d1024/e4/lr5e-4/use_norm0, dirname-verified) DOES NOT REPRODUCE published row.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from TSLibAudit/results/*ITP_PEMS04_96*; config verified in result dirname (dm1024_el4_df1024)
results.note: EXACT official script (d1024/e4/lr5e-4/use_norm0, dirname-verified) DOES NOT REPRODUCE published row. One seed diverged (0.709). Official config WORSE than canonical d256 (0.3115) at this horizon.
Reproduces (0.0679 vs 0.067) - PEMS07 short-horizon row is honest; the failure is PEMS04-specific so far.

PUBLISHED-REGIME PEMS04 GRID COMPLETE: 0.0726/0.0843/0.1006/0.1235 vs paper 0.076/0.084/0.115/0.137 - fully reproducible, uniformly slightly favorable. Takes the pred-96 cell lead (vs CrossFormer canonical 0.1310, DMamba pub 0.143). Canonical-config regime starting now.

DECOMPOSITION: published-config 0.0726 -> canonical 0.0770 = 6% capacity/tuning premium. Same-budget ladder: CrossFormer 0.0679 < StructMamba 0.0738 < S-Mamba 0.0770 < MultiRel 0.0807 < iT 0.0887. StructMamba beats S-Mamba on equal footing at this cell.

Capacity premium grows with horizon: 6% at pred-12 -> 17% at pred-24 (0.0843 pub-config vs 0.0987 canonical). Same-budget ladder: CrossFormer 0.0783 < StructMamba 0.0919 < S-Mamba 0.0987 < MultiRel 0.1055 < iT 0.1248. StructMamba wins again on equal footing.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from TSLibAudit/results/*ITP_PEMS07_24* (2 of 3 seeds visible at harvest; recheck)
results.note: Exact-script = canonical d256 (0.0969); published 0.088 ~10% favorable - mild non-reproduction.

DIVERGED 3/3 SEEDS at N=883 with iT's OWN official config - second adversarial divergence (after PEMS03-96 d512). Published 0.110 unreachable; canonical d256 converges stably (0.1572). Full-attention instability at scale, exactly as the StructMamba/MultiRel stability analysis predicts.

DECISIVE: capacity/tuning premium 31% at pred-48 (pub-config 0.1006 vs canonical 0.1450); premium ladder 6%/17%/31% across horizons. Same-budget: StructMamba 0.1297 WINS by 11%, then S-Mamba 0.1450 < MultiRel 0.1589 < CrossFormer 0.1609 < iT 0.1988. The published Mamba long-horizon advantage is substantially a capacity artifact.

results.provenance: metrics.npy[1] from SDMamba/results/SMCAN_P04_96_*_{0,1,2}; saved before disk-full OSError (post-metrics pred-array write failed, harmless)
results.note: TWO-REGIME PEMS04 GRID COMPLETE. Premium ladder final: 6%/17%/31%/81%. StructMamba (0.1974) beats canonical S-Mamba (0.2240) by 12% - StructMamba wins ALL FOUR same-budget cells.

THIRD official-config divergence (3/3 seeds, MSE 1.7-1.9). iT official configs now diverge at PEMS03-96, PEMS07-48, PEMS07-96. Canonical d256 converges at all of these (0.2485 here). Published long-horizon iT rows are unreachable from the official scripts in our environment.
Official config (d512) WORSE than canonical d256 (0.0794) and misses published 0.079 by 10%.
Official config worse than canonical (0.1152) and misses published by 17%. PEMS08 joins PEMS04: published rows unreachable, canonical config superior to official config.
FOURTH official-config divergence (3/3 seeds). Divergence set now: PEMS03-96, PEMS07-48, PEMS07-96, PEMS08-48. Canonical d256 converges at all (0.2011 here).

FIFTH and final official-config divergence. ITP sweep COMPLETE. Verdict: iT official configs reproduce only short-horizon cells; diverge at 5 long-horizon cells across 3 datasets; canonical d256 never diverges. Published long-horizon iT rows (incl. the table everyone copies) are not regenerable from released code in our environment.
30% seed spread
55% seed spread; published favorable but within its own variance
published favorable 8%; tight seeds here
published favorable 4%

published_reference.source: S-Mamba table lists TimesNet P07 only at some horizons; comparison: StructMamba 0.1185, MultiRel 0.1356, iT-canonical 0.1572

results.note: TimesNet at N=883 long horizon; behind StructMamba 0.1185 and MultiRel 0.1356. Also running on 5060 (audit_P07) as cross-box check.
Channel-independent PatchTST mid-pack on PEMS03; far behind channel-attention methods - confirms cross-channel structure matters at this N.

results.config: SE-masked CrossFormer (cross-time attn + SE-masked cross-dimension; canonical d256/e3)

published_reference.source: PHASE-2; vs baseline CrossFormer 0.0679 (60-75% spread), StructMamba 0.0738

results.note: HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED at pred-12: matches baseline CrossFormer accuracy (0.0687 vs 0.0679) with seed spread 0.9% vs CrossFormer's 40-75%. SE structure preserves accuracy, kills the variance. Beats StructMamba (0.0738) and all canonical methods on this cell.
CLEAN rerun confirms shared-box value (0.1332 vs 0.1324). Tight 3% spread. On small-N PEMS08, CrossFormer-family weak vs channel-attention (iT 0.0794) regardless of SE masks.

published_reference.source: two-regime audit; S-Mamba WINS vs StructMamba 0.3026

results.note: At N=883, TimesNet behind all channel-attention methods.
35% seed spread (0.33-0.47); published 0.226 unreachable. TimesNet also unstable at long horizon. StructMamba 0.3026, S-Mamba-can 0.2701 both better+tighter.
39% seed spread; vs StructMamba 0.1247 (tight). TimesNet variance grows with horizon on P03 too.

published_reference.source: PHASE-2 clean; baseline CrossFormer 0.1637; tracks it tight (3% spread). StructMamba 0.1024 far better on small-N.

results.note: StructMamba 0.1733 best at N=883 long horizon.
Channel-independent; tight seeds but far behind channel-attention. Cross-channel structure decisive at N=358 long horizon.

BEATS ALL canonical methods AND S-Mamba-d1024 at this horizon. pred-48: SEC 0.0909 < S-Mamba-pub 0.1006 < StructMamba 0.1297 < baseline-CrossFormer 0.1609(unstable). SE masks both stabilize AND improve CrossFormer's long-horizon accuracy. Tight ~2% seed spread.

published_reference.source: PHASE-2 clean; KEYSTONE: beats S-Mamba-d1024 0.1235, baseline-CrossFormer 0.1310(75%spread), StructMamba 0.1974 - canonical budget, tight 2% spread
PHASE-2 clean; BEATS StructMamba 0.3026, S-Mamba-can 0.2701, S-Mamba-pub 0.2659 - best on PEMS08 long horizon, tight
PHASE-2; baseline CrossFormer P03-12 0.0636(seed-lottery), StructMamba 0.0635, iT 0.0694

results.note: PEMS03 extension. Competitive (~ties StructMamba 0.0635, beats iT 0.0694), tight 4% spread.
Channel-independent, tight but far behind channel-attention at long horizon - PatchTST P03 grid complete.
Channel-independent collapses at long horizon (0.50 at pred-96) - SECrossformer 0.1087 is 4.6x better. Strongest evidence yet that cross-channel structure is essential.

~ties StructMamba 0.0839, beats iT 0.1002; tight 2.5% seed spread vs baseline CrossFormer's lottery.

At N=883 short-horizon, SEC (0.0716) trails StructMamba 0.0596 & channel-attention methods, but tighter than baseline CrossFormer (44% spread). CrossFormer-family weaker than iT-family at large-N short horizon; long horizons (48/96) are the test.
18% seed spread; TimesNet P03 grid complete. StructMamba 0.1878 far better+tighter.

methodology_notes: drop_last check (2026-06-12): our audit tree evaluates drop_last=False (post-TFB); SDMamba evaluates batch_size=1 at test where its drop_last=True is harmless. Both pipelines cover every test window - S-Mamba reproductions and our canonical numbers are directly comparable. The iTransformer PEMS04 non-reproduction is NOT explained by the drop_last bug.
